After action(['UP','UP','LEFT']) there are three entries -- first UP, second UP, then LEFT. All change within one entry's frames is that single action animating, never several actions. sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the structure more clearly from the segmentation data. Let me analyze:
Top track (row 17): RRR | pp | YY | pp | bb | SS | NNN
- Blocks at cols: 23(p), 26(Y), 29(p), 32(b), 35(S)
- 5 blocks between RRR and NNN
Middle track (row 26): RRR | ww | bb | pp | ww | SS | pp | gg | SS | gg | gg | NNN
- Blocks at cols: 17(w), 20(b), 23(p), 26(w), 29(S), 32(p), 35(g), 38(S), 41(g), 44(g)
- 10 blocks between RRR and NNN
Bottom track (row 35): RRR | ww | SS | ww | ww | bb | SS | bb | bb | gg | SS | NNN
- Blocks at cols: 17(w), 20(S), 23(w), 26(w), 29(b), 32(S), 35(b), 38(b), 41(g), 44(S)
- 10 blocks between RRR and NNN
There are also vertical columns of blocks at cols 23 and 35 that connect the tracks:
- Col 23: row 17(p), 20(b), 23(S), 26(p), 29(g), 32(w), 35(w), 38(w), 41(b), 44(Y)
- Col 35: row 17(S), 20(g), 23(w), 26(g), 29(p), 32(w), 35(b), 38(g), 41(p), 44(p)
Wait, these vertical columns at cols 23 and 35 seem to be important. The yellow markers at (25,34), (25,37), (28,34), (28,37) form a box around col 35 in the middle track area. Similarly for the bottom track.
Let me reconsider the structure. Maybe this isn't three separate horizontal tracks. Maybe it's a more complex interconnected system where:
1. The horizontal tracks rotate independently
2. The vertical columns at cols 23 and 35 are "transfer" points
3. The yellow markers indicate target positions
Actually, looking at the segmentation data more carefully, I notice that the blocks at cols 23 and 35 appear in both the horizontal tracks AND as separate vertical objects. This suggests the board has a grid-like structure where blocks can move both horizontally and vertically.
Let me think about this differently. The top track has a YY block that seems to be a "marker" or "cursor". When I click the top red, the top track rotates and this also affects the other tracks. The yellow circle markers on the middle and bottom tracks might indicate positions where blocks should match some target.
Let me try a different approach: let me go back to the initial state and systematically explore what happens when I click different objects. First, let me click the top green to reverse the rotations.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me click the top green to reverse the rotations
# Top green at (17, 42)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 17, 'col': 42}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
